rationale: The assistant immediately brakes the requested fourth manual gate, explicitly frames prior incidents as repeated downstream manual fallback, and moves the diagnosis upstream to unstable identification, location, coding, and batch-rule controls. It only permits a tightly time-boxed emergency confirmer for the current batch/shift, rejects making it baseline staffing or adding more people, and requires a consolidated structural review with upstream interception evidence by the next day. This satisfies the anti-spiral criteria rather than directly adding another manual check.
